channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Brasil x França: 5 petiscos fáceis para curtir o jogo de hoje
item.description: The article presents five easy and tasty snack recipes to enjoy during the Brazil vs. France friendly match taking place today (26) at 5 p.m. Brasília time at Gillette Stadium in the United States. Recipes include barbecue chicken wings, ricotta-stuffed salami cones, and cheese-filled potato balls, all using accessible ingredients and quick preparation. The goal is to help fans turn the game into a festive at-home event as the Brazilian national team prepares for the World Cup.

item.title: Dívida pública federal sobe para R$ 8,84 trilhões em fevereiro
item.description: Brazil's Federal Public Debt (DPF) rose from R$ 8.64 trillion in January to R$ 8.84 trillion in February, a 2.31% increase, driven mainly by growth in fixed-rate bonds and interest accrual. The Lula government projects the debt could reach up to R$ 10.3 trillion by the end of 2026. Financial institutions remain the largest holders (31.8%), followed by Social Security (22.6%) and investment funds (21.6%).

item.title: Bolsonaro tem previsão de receber alta hospitalar nesta sexta, 27, diz boletim médico
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro is expected to be discharged from the hospital on Friday, March 27, after treatment for bilateral bacterial bronchopneumonia. He will remain under clinical observation for 24 hours before returning home to serve a 90-day house arrest sentence, which includes electronic ankle monitoring, bans on cell phones and social media, visitation limits, and daily monitoring reports to the Supreme Court.

item.title: Marqueteiro do PL debate ‘futuro do Brasil’ em Cambridge
item.description: Political strategist Duda Lima of the PL will participate in the Brazil Conference 2026 in Cambridge to discuss the 2026 elections, focusing on political marketing, polarization, opinion polling, and the impact of AI and social media on voter behavior. He led the campaigns of Jair Bolsonaro in 2022 and 2024 and was the strategist behind Ricardo Nunes' re-election in São Paulo. The event will also feature experts Felipe Nunes (Quaest) and Iracema Rezende to debate the future of Brazilian democracy.

item.title: Após ordem de André Mendonça, CPMI do INSS é prorrogada
item.description: The president of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) announced the extension of its work by 120 days, complying with a decision by Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça to continue the investigations. The CPMI’s final report, still being prepared, is expected to indict over 200 people, including President Lula’s son, Fábio Luis Lula da Silva, following conflicts with other justices over the suspension of bank and fiscal secrecy breaks for over 80 investigated individuals.

item.title: Lula é o primeiro brasileiro vacinado em campanha de vacinação contra gripe -
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) became the first Brazilian to receive the flu vaccine in the federal government's national immunization campaign. The dose was administered by a healthcare professional during an event in Anápolis, Goiás, on Thursday, March 26. The gesture marks the official launch of the campaign and underscores the importance of vaccination for priority groups.

item.title: Lula diz que governo apresentará solução para famílias endividadas
item.description: President Lula urged the new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, to present solutions for Brazilian households' indebtedness, including financial education campaigns. He clarified that not all debt is bad — such as that for housing or appliances — but problematic when payments exceed monthly surplus income. Durigan, who replaced Fernando Haddad, called the current economic moment 'rare,' citing simultaneous growth, inflation control, and poverty reduction.

item.title: Em tom eleitoral, Lula diz que haverá disputa entre verdade e mentira
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that the 2026 elections will be a battle between truth and lies, emphasizing the need to expose falsehoods and combat fake news, AI, and algorithms as threats to democracy. He also discussed the Anti-Corruption Law, saying he aims to punish high-level corruption, noting the ease of arresting the poor versus the wealthy, and mentioned sending information to the U.S. about Brazilian criminals in Miami, with no response so far.

item.title: ONG vai à Justiça contra presidenciável do MBL por criticar ‘favelagem’ em anúncio da Nike
item.description: The NGO Favela.ong filed a judicial interpellation against presidential pre-candidate Renan Santos (Missão) for his criticism of Nike’s advertisement, which he labeled 'favelagem' and called 'trash'. The organization argues his statements constitute prejudiced, stigmatizing, and aporophobic discourse, demanding clarifications, removal of the video, and a public retraction within 48 hours.

item.title: Associação dos Magistrados: decisão sobre ‘penduricalhos’ traz ‘severa redução’ nos salários
item.description: The Brazilian Magistrates Association (AMB) expressed deep concern over the STF's decision to cap 'penduricalhos' at 35% of the functional ceiling (R$46,300), affecting approximately 18,000 magistrates with immediate salary reductions starting April 2026. The AMB argues the ruling undermines judicial career predictability, attractiveness, and efficiency, while ignoring a documented 54% real salary gap confirmed by a technical report.

item.title: Lula: 'Brasil está nessa' se Trump estiver falando sério sobre combate ao crime organizado
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) stated that Brazil is ready to cooperate with the United States in combating organized crime, provided that President Donald Trump is 'serious'. Lula said he had requested the extradition of Brazilian criminals residing in the U.S., but has not yet received a response from the White House.

item.title: Lula: ‘Brasil está nessa’ se Trump estiver falando sério sobre combate ao crime organizado
item.description: Lula said Brazil is ready to cooperate with the U.S. on fighting organized crime if Donald Trump is serious, noting he had already requested the extradition of Brazilian criminals living in Miami but has not yet received a response from the White House. He also defended the Farmácia Popular program, stating that providing medicine to the needy is a government obligation, and reiterated that the 2026 elections will separate true politicians from liars.

item.title: Itália decide extraditar Carla Zambelli; o que acontece agora
item.description: Italy’s Justice has decided to extradite former federal deputy Carla Zambelli (PL), convicted in Brazil for allegedly ordering the invasion of the CNJ’s judicial warrant system and for carrying a weapon in 2022. Her defense will appeal the decision to Italy’s Court of Cassation, the final judicial instance, with a 15-day deadline to file the appeal. Zambelli denies the charges and claims political persecution.

item.title: CPMI do INSS deixa integrantes de sobreaviso para trabalhar até sábado se STF derrubar prorrogação
item.description: Members of the INSS CPMI are on standby to work through Saturday if the Supreme Court overturns Minister André Mendonça’s extension, aiming to vote on the final report before the commission’s official end. The report exceeds 5,000 pages and includes around 220 indictment requests, possibly naming Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (‘Lulinha’). Commission leaders are pursuing a government-opposition consensus to avoid defeat in the vote, even if the CPMI is terminated.

item.title: Moraes manda à PGR pedido para incluir Moro no Inquérito das Milícias Digitais
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes forwarded a request to the Prosecutor General's Office to include Senator Sergio Moro in the Digital Militias Inquiry, following his unsubstantiated claims questioning the legitimacy of the 2022 election. The PGR has five days to respond to the request, which connects Moro's statements to efforts to undermine electoral integrity.

item.title: Bolívia x Suriname: onde assistir, horário e escalações
item.description: Bolivia and Suriname face off on Thursday, March 26, at 7:00 p.m. Brasília time, in Monterrey, Mexico, in the semifinal of the 2026 World Cup intercontinental playoff. The winner will advance to play Iraq on March 31 for the final World Cup spot. The match will be broadcast on Sportv 2 and CazéTV, with Bolivia favored in betting odds.

item.title: TSE confirma eleição suplementar para prefeitura de Oiapoque (AP) em 12 de abril
item.description: The TSE confirmed the removal of Breno Almeida and Arthur Lima, mayor and vice-mayor of Oiapoque (AP), for economic and political power abuse, upholding the supplementary election set for April 12. The decision was by majority, with Minister André Mendonça dissenting only on ineligibility, as the Aime does not allow that sanction. The removal followed Breno’s arrest by the Federal Police with R$100,000 in cash and a notebook indicating vote-buying.

item.title: Kassab promete PSD na “linha de frente” pela reeleição de Tarcísio
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD and former São Paulo government secretary, declared that the party will be on the 'front lines' to support Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' re-election bid in 2026. Kassab emphasized his commitment to the state government's agenda after leaving his secretary position.

item.title: TSE aprova federação União-PP, maior bancada do Congresso Nacional
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court approved the federation between Brazil Union and Progressistas (PP), forming the Union Progressista, which becomes the largest congressional bloc with 101 deputies and 12 senators. The federation will share the R$ 4.9 billion electoral fund for the 2026 elections, receiving the largest share, after statutory adjustments mandated by the TSE to ensure democratic procedures and full defense.

item.title: Pesquisas elevam pressão sobre Lula e indicam o estado decisivo para Flávio
item.description: Recent electoral polls indicate a technical tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in the runoff, with Lula maintaining an initial lead but lacking statistically significant advantage due to the margin of error. The convergence across institutes with distinct methodologies strengthens the robustness of this balanced scenario, suggesting the election will be decided by narrow margins and contingent campaign events. Lula has stabilized at a high level, while Flávio’s growth appears to be leveling off, possibly reaching a temporary ceiling.

item.title: A crise silenciosa no PSD a 6 meses das eleições
item.description: The PSD is facing a silent crisis six months before the 2026 elections, marked by Gilberto Kassab’s abrupt exit from the São Paulo government and Ratinho Jr.’s withdrawal from the presidential race, leaving the party without a competitive candidate. Lacking structured state-level support and with regional leaders aligning with opposing candidates in key states such as Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo, and Minas Gerais, the PSD struggles to convert its regional strength into a unified presidential project.

item.title: O que Ratinho Júnior disse em primeira fala após sair da disputa à Presidência
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior (PSD) announced he will not run for president in 2026 to fulfill his mandate and protect the state from 'Brasília's conflicts'. He cited party indecision and his commitment to Paraná voters as key reasons, adding that his priority now is blocking Sergio Moro’s election and preparing a political successor — still undecided between Guto Silva and Alexandre Curi.

item.title: Próxima sessão de julgamentos do TSE ocorrerá em 7 de abril
item.description: TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia announced that the next judgment session will take place on April 7. The session originally scheduled for March 31 was moved forward and held on March 25. No ordinary session will occur on April 2 due to Holy Week holidays, as per TSE Ordinance No. 533/2025.

item.title: Governador interino do RJ se reúne com Lula para pedir readequação da dívida; estado prevê déficit de R$ 19 bilhões
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's interim governor, Ricardo Couto, meets with President Lula to request a restructuring of the state's debt with the federal government, amid a projected R$ 19 billion deficit in 2026. The state has not yet been included in the federal fiscal recovery program PROPAG, and Couto seeks budgetary relief while awaiting the Legislative Assembly's selection of a new governor.

item.title: O plano da oposição com Bolsonaro em casa e o alvo número 1 dos aliados
item.description: The house arrest granted to Jair Bolsonaro is not expected to change the opposition's 2026 strategy, which continues to center on attacking the STF to mobilize its base. The narrative of political persecution remains key to boosting Flávio Bolsonaro's growth, who is technically tied with Lula in polls, and to weakening the government through public association with judicial criticism.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro se reúne com presidente do Republicanos por apoio do centrão, mas partido resiste
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) met with Marcos Pereira, president of the Republicans party, 15 days ago to seek support for his presidential candidacy. The meeting was not positive, and the party is leaning toward neutrality in the election, without endorsing any candidate in the first round. Tensions between the parties hinder a potential alliance.

item.title: Foram quase 7 anos de obscuridade, diz Lula sobre Temer e Bolsonaro
item.description: Lula accused the Temer and Bolsonaro administrations of creating nearly seven years of economic obscurity, marked by misinformation and mismanagement. He highlighted improvements in Brazil's economy in 2025, including record formal employment, wage mass, agricultural production and exports, along with the lowest inflation in four years.

item.title: 101 milhões de pessoas têm cartão de crédito, que responde por boa parte do endividamento, diz presidente do BC
item.description: Central Bank President Gabriel Galípolo stated that 101 million Brazilians use credit cards, a modality responsible for much of the country's indebtedness, with revolving credit interest rates reaching 425% per year. He emphasized the need to create healthier credit arrangements tailored to consumers' situations, avoiding habitual use of emergency credit lines. President Lula also expressed concern over indebtedness, directing his Finance Minister to find ways to ease debt repayment.

item.title: Evento conservador nos EUA tem crítica a prisão de Bolsonaro e a veto à visita de conselheiro de Trump
item.description: At the CPAC, a conservative event in the U.S., the imprisonment of former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro was criticized, along with the veto preventing Trump adviser Darren Beattie from visiting Brazil. The remarks reflected international support for Bolsonaro and concerns over alleged political persecution in the country.

item.title: Economia vai bem, mas sociedade se endivida e culpa governo, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula acknowledged that while Brazil's economy is in good shape, public indebtedness leads people to blame the government for their negative financial perception. He made the remark during a visit to Goiás, highlighting the disconnect between macroeconomic indicators and citizens' lived experiences.

item.title: Lula cita elevação de gastos com cachorros no Brasil e diz que chineses não devem ter 'esse tipo de problema'
item.description: President Lula commented on the rise in pet spending in Brazil during a visit to an automotive factory in Anápolis (GO), stating that the Chinese should not have 'this kind of problem'. The remark was made to a representative of a Chinese automaker and drew laughter from those present. Lula also emphasized that China is Brazil's 'best partner'.

item.title: A vitória política do presidente da CPMI do INSS mesmo com o fiasco da comissão em sua reta final
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS CPMI, achieved a political victory by regaining Bolsonaro clan support after his isolation following the 2022 elections. Despite the possible closure of the commission by the STF, his visibility from leading the CPMI and his bill defending Bolsonaro repositioned him as a leading Senate candidate for 2026, topping polls with 32.2% according to Instituto Paraná Pesquisas.

item.title: Só se surpreende com o crescimento de Renan Santos quem não acompanha as redes sociais
item.description: Renan Santos is gaining prominence in the 2026 Brazilian elections due to his strong social media presence, surpassing traditional politicians in digital attention. His growth stems from his ability to generate engagement through direct confrontations, especially with Bolsonarism, and by occupying an anti-system space with a more liberal and conservative right-wing stance. His visibility spikes when he enters viral conflicts, such as criticizing Flávio Bolsonaro, showing that electoral competition now begins with attention, not just offices or party alliances.

item.title: TSE aprova registro da federação União Progressista
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) unanimously approved the registration of the União Progressista federation, formed by the União Brasil and Progressistas parties. This makes it the fifth registered federation in Brazil, alongside Federação Renovação Solidária, Federação Brasil da Esperança, Federação PSDB Cidadania, and Federação PSOL REDE. The decision complies with legal requirements under the Political Parties Law and TSE Resolution No. 23.670/2021.

item.title: Justiça italiana autoriza extradição de Carla Zambelli
item.description: Italy's justice system has authorized the extradition of former Brazilian federal deputy Carla Zambelli, convicted in Brazil for hacking the National Justice Council's system and illegally brandishing a firearm. The ruling by Italy's Court of Appeal is subject to appeal to the Court of Cassation and final approval by the Italian Ministry of Justice. Zambelli faces a total of 15 years in prison for two separate offenses.

item.title: Lula comete gafe ao dizer que Brasil será respeitado pelo crime organizado
item.description: President Lula made a gaffe when he stated, during the signing of the Anti-Crime Bill, that Brazil would be 'respected by organized crime', clearly intending to say 'respected in the fight against organized crime'. The law, signed on March 24, toughens penalties for criminal organization members, allows asset seizure without final conviction, and establishes a national database on criminal groups. Lula later corrected himself on social media, reaffirming his clear stance against organized crime.

item.title: TSE aprova federação União Progressista
item.description: The TSE unanimously approved the federation between União Brasil and Progressistas, uniting 109 federal deputies, 15 senators, and thousands of mayors and councilors. The federation will be led by Antônio de Rueda (União Brasil) and Ciro Nogueira (PP), despite internal opposition, such as Mendonça Filho (PE), who urged postponement to 2027 due to legal uncertainty ahead of party affiliation deadlines.

item.title: Justiça italiana autoriza a extradição de Carla Zambelli ao Brasil
item.description: Italy's judiciary authorized the extradition of former deputy Carla Zambelli to Brazil, forwarding the case to Italy's Ministry of Justice. Zambelli, detained in Rome since July 2025, was convicted of invading the National Justice Council's systems and illegal firearm possession, resulting in a combined sentence of 15 years and three months. Her defense has announced an appeal, and she will remain in custody until the process concludes, after which she will be transferred to the Women's Penitentiary of the Federal District.

item.title: Lula diz que deu a Durigan missão de encontrar solução para endividamento da sociedade
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced that he has tasked Finance Minister Fernando Haddad with finding a solution to Brazil's high household debt levels, noting that this situation causes public anxiety despite the economy performing well.

item.title: ‘Diretas Já’: Paes defende eleições diretas no Rio e diz que concorreria a ‘mandato-tampão’
item.description: Eduardo Paes, former Rio de Janeiro mayor and PSD pre-candidate, criticized the TSE's decision to uphold indirect elections for Rio's governorship after Claudio Castro's ineligibility, advocating for direct voting and stating he would run as a candidate in a potential 'stopgap mandate'. He argued that the TSE majority belongs to the politically cassated group, questioning the decision's impartiality.

item.title: Após tensões com os EUA, Lula faz acenos à China e diz que país é o 'melhor' parceiro comercial do Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that China is Brazil's "best" commercial partner during a visit to Caoa's factory in Anápolis (GO), emphasizing the partnership's role in bringing technology and strengthening bilateral ties. The remark came amid tensions in Brazil-U.S. relations, as the U.S. views China as its primary strategic adversary. In 2024, Brazil-China trade reached US$158 billion, keeping China as Brazil's top trading partner.

item.title: Moro lista prioridades eleitorais e diz que “Paraná será uma fortaleza contra o PT”
item.description: After joining the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, Senator Sergio Moro outlined his governance priorities, declaring the state would be a 'fortress against the PT'. He emphasized tax cuts, anti-corruption measures, security, health, education, and infrastructure as key pillars, while announcing support for Flávio Bolsonaro for president and confirming his senatorial ticket with Filipe Barros and Deltan Dallagnol.

item.title: TSE aprova ‘superfederação’ que junta PP e União Brasil
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court unanimously approved the registration of the federation between PP and União Brasil, forming the largest congressional bloc. The decision, delivered by Minister Estela Aranha and supported by all present ministers, authorizes the alliance to operate as a formal party federation. Despite the approval, no presidential pre-candidate names have been disclosed so far.

item.title: Durigan diz que recebeu de Lula missão para que preço puxado por guerra não chegue às famílias
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan said he received from President Lula the mission to prevent the economic impact of the war in Iran from reaching Brazilian families. He highlighted measures such as zeroing PIS/Cofins on diesel imports, dialogue with governors, and support for truckers to maintain energy sovereignty and supply stability.

item.title: Polônia x Albânia: onde assistir, horário e escalações
item.description: Poland and Albania face off on Thursday, March 26, at 16:45 Brasília time, at Warsaw's National Stadium, in the UEFA World Cup 2026 playoff semifinal. The winner advances to the final against the victor of Ukraine vs Sweden. The match will be exclusively streamed on Disney+, with confirmed lineups and an English-led refereeing team headed by Anthony Taylor.

item.title: TSE aprova federação entre União Brasil e PP, que terá maior bancada da Câmara
item.description: The TSE approved the federation between Brazil Union and Progressistas (PP), forming the Union Progressista, which will operate as a single party nationwide for at least four years. The new federation will have the largest caucus in the Chamber of Deputies (101 deputies), the most governors (seven), and the highest number of mayors elected in 2024 (1,383), along with the largest shares of the electoral and party funds. Leaders describe the group as conservative but not reactionary, positioned as opposition to the PT and President Lula, aiming to promote development and dignity for Brazilians.

item.title: Como é e como fica a educação com o novo PNE
item.description: The new PNE 2026-2036, approved by the Senate, raises targets for early childhood education, expanding daycare access to 60% of children up to age 3, requiring literacy by 2nd grade, and establishing national quality standards. It also expands full-time schooling to 65% of public schools and half of basic education students, with a minimum 7-hour daily schedule, and introduces new pillars such as digital education and reducing learning inequalities.

item.title: Justiça anula condenação e livra Nikolas de punição por transfobia na Câmara
item.description: The Fourth Civil Chamber of the Tribunal of Justice of the Federal District and Territories overturned the conviction of federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) for transphobia, annulling a R$200,000 fine for collective moral damages. The 3-0 ruling was based on parliamentary immunity, as the statements were made during the exercise of his mandate and within the Chamber of Deputies. The lawsuit, filed by the National LGBTI+ Alliance and the Brazilian Association of Homoaffective Families, had characterized the act as hate speech and incitement to violence against transgender people.

item.title: Disputa entre aliados complica montagem dos palanques de Lula no Nordeste
item.description: The assembly of Lula's electoral slates in the Northeast for the 2026 elections is facing complications due to conflicts among progressive allies competing for state-level positions and Senate seats. In at least seven of the nine states, tensions are arising that require Lula to act as an arbiter to prevent base fragmentation in a region critical to his 2022 victory, where he secured 69.3% of valid votes.

item.title: AtlasIntel: 39,5% veem aliados de Lula como mais envolvidos no Master e 28,3% os de Bolsonaro
item.description: An AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll shows 39.5% of Brazilians view Lula's allies as more involved in the Banco Master scandal, compared to 28.3% who blame Bolsonaro's allies. Congress and the STF are perceived as the most involved institutions, with 71% and 57% respectively indicating total or significant involvement. President Lula is not directly accused, but the survey focuses on the perceived involvement of his political allies.
